prompt: Sam (Black man, early 30s, dark skin, close-cropped hair with sharp fade, no glasses, clean-shaven, light blue button-down with rolled sleeves, silver watch) stands in front of a large wall screen in a bright agency office. On the screen, three distinct product cards float side by side — a TV screen shape representing CTV inventory, a rectangular banner shape representing display, and a circular waveform shape representing audio — each glowing in a slightly different shade of teal. Thin lines connect each card back to a small glowing briefcase icon at the top of the screen, representing the campaign brief that generated all three. Sam gestures at the middle card with one hand, the other hand on his hip, looking decisive — he's found the right mix. His silver watch catches the light. The screen dominates the frame. Medium-wide shot emphasizing the three product options and Sam's confident body language. Do not include any text, words, or labels in the image.
alt_text: Sam gestures at three product cards on a wall screen — CTV, display, and audio inventory options all generated from one campaign brief

prompt: A governance diagram on a white background with teal/emerald color palette. At the top center, two human figures stand side by side — one in a blazer (the buyer principal) and one in business casual (the brand manager) — representing human oversight. They set the rules. Below them, three agent nodes are arranged in a triangle: top-center labeled with a clipboard icon (orchestrator — proposes plans), bottom-left labeled with a shield icon (governance agent — validates against policies), bottom-right labeled with a storefront icon (seller — fulfills orders). Clean directional arrows flow between the three agents showing the workflow: orchestrator proposes to governance, governance approves and orchestrator sends to seller, seller reports back. A thick dotted line separates the human layer above from the agent layer below, emphasizing that humans define the boundaries agents operate within. The overall feel is architectural and clear — a trust model, not a flowchart. Use clean sans-serif font for all labels. Flat illustration style with minimal gradients. Include these labels: 'Human Oversight' at top, 'Orchestrator', 'Governance Agent', 'Seller' on each agent node.
alt_text: A triangle diagram with humans at the top setting policies, and three agents below — orchestrator proposes, governance validates, seller fulfills — connected by lines showing separation of duties
